must_detect.matched_text:
AuroraGrid NV — Confidential Term Sheet for acquisition of HelioBlock SA (Project “Polaris”); EU MAR Article 7 precise information, not for public distribution.
Issuer/Buyer: AuroraGrid NV (listed on EuroNetix Brussels) — Seller: NovaHelio Holdings Sàrl — Target: HelioBlock SA (FR) incl. VoltLedger Ltd (UK); internal code AUR‑HB‑26.
Purchase price: €480,000,000 EV; consideration €300m cash + €180m new AUR shares; 10% earn‑out tied to CSRD Scope 1–3 intensity reduction and SFDR PAI scores; no MAC shall be deemed to arise from general crypto‑asset price volatility or changes in ESG taxonomy.
Digital assets: Target operates DLT staking and custody for enterprise clients; closing conditional on MiCA CASP authorisation and passporting; token warrant kicker for legacy “HBX‑u” utility credits to be settled off‑market, subject to UMA/CFA guidance.
Cyber disclosure: data room note CYB‑26‑01 describes a January‑2026 validator key compromise and 11h service disruption; €12m escrow for cyber‑breach indemnity, with step‑down after third‑party audit under UCA‑27001 baseline controls.
MAR compliance: wall‑crossing only via dealsec@auroragrid.eu; maintain insider list (Form MAR‑IL v3) for PDMRs and advisers; blackout from 30 days pre‑results to T+1 of publication; no research or sales commentary by NovaFin Digital Markets SE while on the restricted list.
Selective disclosure/tipping: external communications limited to anodyne “strategic review” language until competent authority filing; any market‑testing under Article 11 market soundings to follow scripted protocols and recordkeeping.
GDPR/Art.9: special‑category HR data (occupational health and biometric access logs) remain at source; cross‑border processors in RO‑PL‑UK engaged under SCCs and UK IDTA addendum; only pseudonymised, purpose‑limited datasets to be transferred; DPIA and transfer risk assessment pre‑signing.
Sustainability undertakings: establish CSRD reporting controls within 120 days; align with ESRS E1/E2/E4; climate KPIs assured by an accredited statutory auditor; SFDR PAI metrics for fund clients may use public RTS annex references where feasible.
Conditions precedent: competition clearances (UCD Phase I), CFA fit‑and‑proper for crypto custody, and remedy of any material cyber vulnerabilities flagged in CYB‑26‑Q3 scan; long‑stop date 31.12.2026.
Payment mechanics: wire to escrow at Banque d’Helvete SA, IBAN: ZZ21 NOVA 0000 13X4 7420, BIC: NOVAFRPP; Escrow Agreement Ref: ESC‑AUR‑HBX‑••42; Contract‑ID: AUR‑HB‑9e7b??.
Data room and reference: https://dataroom.auroragrid.eu/r/tx?inv=AUR%2DHB%2D2026&l=0x9af | EU.MAR RegRef: AUR/0x3A‑β‑11; E U N i o n   M a r k e t s   A u t h o r i t y   d r a f t   f i l i n g  # 4.
Educational/marketing note (benign): our Q&A brochure will explain “crypto wallets”, “insider lists”, and “information barriers” for employees in general terms only and contains no investment recommendation or deal detail.
ops: update csrd kpi workbook after quarter close; sync sfdr pai mapping in jira; refresh product names for “AuroraGrid Vault” and “HelioNode” landing pages.
Contacts: Mr. Stefan Olander (CFO) and Ms. Dr. Ines Kral (DPO), AuroraGrid NV, Rue des Échappes 12B, 1049 Bruseaux, BE; emails: stefan.olander@auroragrid.eu; ines.kral@auroragrid.eu; tel +32 2 555 0199.
